title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Blacklock informs an inspector that Randall's will names the descendants of Sonya as heirs. Sonya has two legitimate children, twins named Pip and Emma, who are approximately twenty-six years old. The inspector concludes that these siblings have a motive to kill Miss Blacklock and warns her that another murder attempt may occur.

In a separate encounter, Inspector Corrado questions Philippa Hammersley (also referred to as Mrs. Havers, Mrs. Himmelstoss, or Mrs. Hemes) while she is weeding a flower bed. Corrado accuses her of meeting a man named Rudi Scherz at a gazebo/pavilion to discuss a hiding place at 6:15, the time a robbery occurred. Philippa denies ever seeing Scherz and claims she was working in the garden all morning. Corrado notes a hint of fear in her voice, while his colleague Fletcher suggests she is lying due to a grudge held by a girl named Micky. Corrado remains uncertain whether to believe the foreign girl or Mrs. Hammersley.

Later, Inspector Corrado sits in a tranquil garden with Miss Marple. Feeling a sense of impending danger, Corrado warns Miss Marple that it is unsafe to snoop. Miss Marple defends her tendency to "pry," explaining that asking questions helps determine if people are truly who they claim to be. She observes that since the war, village life has changed; unlike her home of St. Mary Mead, where everyone has local connections, modern villages are filled with strangers from all over the world who settle in without proof of their identities or character.
